rows.description: Computus Rectoris a 2º die Novem: bris, 1770 ad eundem diem revolutum 1771.
rows.notes: Adopted gemini-flash's comprehensive description for this multi-line header. gpt-5-mini split this into two separate rows (R1 and R2), but visually it forms a single logical header block. The text is very clear on the page.
rows.section_header: Computus Rectoris a 2º die Novem: bris, 1770 ad eundem diem revolutum 1771.

rows.notes: Both extractors identified 'Arrearagia recepta'. Adopted gemini-flash's segmentation of this as a distinct header. gpt-5-mini combined it with 'L s D', but visually it stands alone.

Both extractors correctly identified the column labels. Adopted gpt-5-mini's 'L s D' for accurate capitalization of the pence column header ('D' is clearly capitalized in the image), over gemini-flash's 'L s d'.

rows.description: Longwittenham pro Dim An. ad fest Mich. 1769
rows.notes: Consensus on all monetary values and fraction. Adopted gemini-flash's more complete description, which includes '1769' as visible in the image.
rows.english_description: Half-year rent due at Michaelmas 1769 from the Long Wittenham estate.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year rent due at Michaelmas 1769 from the Long Wittenham estate, amounting to £33 2s 11¼d.

rows.description: S. Kensington Rect. pro Dim. D Mich. 1770
rows.notes: Consensus on monetary values. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'S. Kensington Rect. pro Dim. D Mich. 1770' after visual verification, as it accurately matches the handwritten text. gpt-5-mini's 'S. Navington Rect. pro dim. ad Mich. 1770' had minor inaccuracies in the place name and abbreviations.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year payment at Michaelmas 1770 from the Kensington rectory, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus on monetary values. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Yarlington Rect. pro Tres. & Dim.' based on visual verification of the initial 'Y' and the abbreviation 'Tres.' (Terrem for three), which appeared more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Garrington Rect. pro Trien. & dim.'.
rows.english_description: Payment from the Yarlington rectory covering three years and a half.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the Yarlington rectory covering three years and a half, amounting to £35 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus on monetary values. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Merton pro Anno' as 'Merton' is clearly written, rather than gpt-5-mini's 'Merston'.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent from Merton, amounting to £6 16s 4d.

rows.notes: Consensus on monetary values. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Burden pro Quadiien.' as a direct transcription of the visible text, even though 'Quadrien.' (gpt-5-mini) might be a more common abbreviation for quadriennial. The transcription prioritizes visual fidelity.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadrennial payment from Burden, amounting to £15 15s 4d.

rows.notes: Full consensus on description and all monetary values, including the fraction.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Quadrennial payment from Minchin, amounting to £2 14s 10¾d.

rows.notes: Consensus on monetary values. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Kidlington Mills pro An & Dim.' as it correctly capitalized 'Dim.' matching the image.
rows.english_description: Payment from the Kidlington mills covering one and a half years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the Kidlington mills covering one and a half years, amounting to £10 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Full consensus on all values for this subtotal row.

Pounds, shillings, and whole pence values are consistent. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Garsington Sadler pro An.' for correct spelling. For the fraction, gemini-flash identified 0.75 while gpt-5-mini identified 0.5 with a note about faintness. Upon visual verification, the symbol after the '3' pence clearly resembles a '¾' (0.75), although it is less distinct than other fractions on the page.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from the Garsington tenant Sadler.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from the Garsington tenant Sadler, amounting to £5 13s 3¾d.

rows.notes: Adopted gemini-flash's description which includes the visible leading dash. There was a disagreement on the pounds amount (gemini-flash: 5, gpt-5-mini: 3) and fraction (gemini-flash: 0.75, gpt-5-mini: 0.25). Visual verification clearly shows the pounds amount as '5'. The fraction symbol again closely matches '¾' (0.75), consistent with the preceding row, not '¼' (0.25).
rows.english_description: Annual payment from the Garsington tenant Molineux.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from the Garsington tenant Molineux, amounting to £5 13s 3¾d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Quinquennial payment from Tintinhulle tenant Napper, amounting to £25 0s 11½d.

rows.description: Et chaq. Annuity pro Men. D Mich. 1770
rows.notes: Consensus on monetary values. However, descriptions varied significantly ('Et chaq. Annuity pro Men. D' from gemini-flash vs 'Episcop: Annuity pro their. ad' from gpt-5-mini). Upon close visual verification, gemini-flash's transcription of 'Et chaq.' and 'Men. D' is more accurate to the handwritten text.
rows.english_description: Various annuities due at Michaelmas 1770 (aggregate annuity receipts).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Various annuities due at Michaelmas 1770 (aggregate annuity receipts), amounting to £150 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Full consensus on all values for this final total row.

rows.description: Vide bad Anrears in former Book
rows.notes: There was disagreement on the description ('Anrears' vs 'answers') and row type ('entry' vs 'header'). Upon visual verification, 'Anrears' (likely shorthand for Arrears, meaning overdue payments) is a more accurate transcription of the faded text and makes more sense in a ledger context than 'answers'. The row is a specific marginal note, therefore 'entry' is a more appropriate row type than 'header'.
rows.english_description: See the bad arrears recorded in the former ledger book.
rows.english_description_with_amount: See the bad arrears recorded in the former ledger book.
